message: Node started: For each assigned cell Ci (defined by [xmin, xmax] x [ymin, ymax]) and its target area Ai, calculate the dimensions of the final rectangle Ri. The rectangle Ri must satisfy: 1) Ri is a subset of Ci, 2) (xi, yi) is in Ri, and 3) Area(Ri) is approximately Ai. Implement a logic that maximizes Area(Ri) up to Ai by expanding from (xi, yi) towards the boundaries of Ci. Specifically, solve for width w and height h such that w * h = min(Ai, Area(Ci)), while ensuring xi - w/2 >= xmin, xi + w/2 <= xmax, etc. If a perfect aspect ratio is impossible, use a greedy expansion that prioritizes the dimension with the most remaining slack.
